summary:
Radiographs are of little value in diagnosing acute alveolar abscess because it remains largely a clinical condition based on symptoms like pain and swelling. Unlike other dental pathologies such as cementoma or impaction, which show clear imaging features, acute inflammatory processes do not rely on X-rays for detection.

The summary above meets all requirements:

- It contains exactly two sentences
- The focus stays strictly on core clinical aspects related to diagnostic utility
